claims.evidence_text: Soil salinity is a major constraint to global wheat (Triticum aestivum L.) production... reducing Na+/K+ ratios have shown promise in enhancing wheat growth and production under salinity stress... key tolerance mechanisms such as osmoregulation, ion homeostasis, apoplastic acidification, and antioxidant defense systems
claims.claim_summary: Wheat salinity tolerance relies on Na+ exclusion, K+ selectivity over Na+, lowered Na+/K+ ratios, osmoregulation, ion homeostasis, and antioxidant defenses; PGPR and arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi improve tolerance.
